annotations.label: Partition of an interval

anchor.snippet: A partition of an interval

annotations.note: Mathlib only has partitions of n-dimensional boxes (`BoxIntegral.Prepartition` with predicate `IsPartition`), which specialises to interval partitions.

annotations.label: Sub-interval of a partition

anchor.snippet: is called a sub-interval of the partition

annotations.note: Sub-boxes of a (pre)partition are exposed via `π.boxes : Finset (Box ι)`; no dedicated 1d sub-interval definition.

annotations.label: Mesh (norm) of a partition

anchor.snippet: The mesh or norm of a partition is defined to be the length of the longest sub-interval

annotations.note: Mathlib's BoxIntegral uses `IsSubordinate` w.r.t. a gauge function and `distortion`; there is no mesh/longest-subinterval definition.

annotations.label: Tagged partition

anchor.snippet: A tagged partition

annotations.note: `BoxIntegral.TaggedPrepartition` plus `IsPartition` generalises tagged interval partitions to n dimensions.

annotations.label: Refinement of a tagged partition

anchor.snippet: is a refinement of

annotations.note: Refinement is encoded via `infPrepartition` and the partial order on `Prepartition I`, not as a named `Refinement` definition.

annotations.label: Directed set of tagged partitions

anchor.snippet: We can turn the set of all tagged partitions into a directed set

annotations.note: Mathlib bypasses the directed-set picture and works with the filter `IntegrationParams.toFilter` of subordinate tagged partitions.

annotations.label: Riemann sum

anchor.snippet: The Riemann sum of f with respect to a tagged partition

annotations.note: `BoxIntegral.integralSum f vol π` is the Riemann sum (with a general box-additive `vol`) generalising the 1d Riemann sum.

annotations.label: Lower and upper Darboux sums

anchor.snippet: Closely related concepts are the lower and upper Darboux sums

annotations.note: Mathlib has no lower/upper Darboux sum definition (the file `Mathlib/Analysis/Calculus/Darboux.lean` is about Darboux's theorem on derivatives).

annotations.label: Darboux sums equal Riemann sums for continuous f

anchor.snippet: the lower and upper Darboux sums for an untagged partition are equal to the Riemann sum for that partition

annotations.note: Without Darboux sums in Mathlib, this equality statement is not formalized.

annotations.label: Darboux integral equivalent to Riemann integral

anchor.snippet: is equivalent to the Riemann integral

annotations.note: No Darboux integral is defined in Mathlib, so its equivalence with the Riemann integral is not formalized.

annotations.label: Riemann integral (epsilon-delta)

anchor.snippet: we say that the Riemann integral of f exists and equals s if the following condition holds

annotations.note: `BoxIntegral.hasIntegral_iff` provides the ε–δ characterisation of `HasIntegral` for the box (generalised Riemann) integral.

annotations.label: Riemann integral (refinement form)

anchor.snippet: Our new definition says that the Riemann integral of f exists and equals s

annotations.note: `BoxIntegral.HasIntegral` is defined as a `Filter.Tendsto` over (sub)tagged partitions with parameter `IntegrationParams.Riemann`.

annotations.label: Equivalence of the two Riemann integral definitions

anchor.snippet: these two definitions are equivalent

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `HasIntegral` filter-theoretically and proves the ε–δ form equivalent via `hasIntegral_iff`.

annotations.label: Constant function 1 on [0,1]

anchor.snippet: be the function which takes the value 1 at every point

annotations.note: `intervalIntegral.integral_const` gives ∫ x in a..b, c = (b-a)•c, covering the example via the Lebesgue interval integral rather than a Riemann calculation.

annotations.label: Indicator of rationals is not Riemann integrable

anchor.snippet: be the indicator function of the rational numbers

annotations.note: No statement that the Dirichlet (rationals) indicator fails to be Riemann/box-integrable was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Indicator of Smith–Volterra–Cantor set

anchor.snippet: let C be the Smith–Volterra–Cantor set

annotations.note: The Smith–Volterra–Cantor set and the (non)integrability of its indicator are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Linearity of the Riemann integral

anchor.snippet: The Riemann integral is a linear transformation

annotations.note: `BoxIntegral.HasIntegral.add` and `BoxIntegral.HasIntegral.smul` establish linearity of the box (Riemann) integral; analogous results exist for `intervalIntegral.integral_add`/`integral_smul`.

annotations.label: Riemann integral as a linear functional

anchor.snippet: this makes the Riemann integral a linear functional on the vector space of Riemann-integrable functions

annotations.note: Mathlib has component lemmas for linearity but no bundled `LinearMap` packaging the Riemann/box integral as a linear functional on integrable functions.

annotations.label: Lebesgue–Vitali characterization of Riemann integrability

anchor.snippet: is Riemann integrable if and only if it is continuous almost everywhere

annotations.note: Searches for `integrable_iff_continuous_ae` / Lebesgue–Vitali in `Mathlib` find nothing; the characterisation is not formalized.

annotations.label: Bounded function with countably many discontinuities is Riemann integrable

anchor.snippet: a bounded function (on a compact interval) with only finitely or countably many discontinuities is Riemann integrable

annotations.note: No statement bounding the discontinuity set by countability to conclude Riemann integrability was found.

annotations.label: Right/left-hand limit sufficient criterion for integrability

anchor.snippet: the existence of a right-hand (or left-hand) limit at every point

annotations.note: Mathlib has no integrability criterion phrased in terms of one-sided limits existing everywhere.

annotations.label: Indicator function Riemann-integrable iff Jordan measurable

anchor.snippet: An indicator function of a bounded set is Riemann-integrable if and only if the set is Jordan measurable

annotations.note: Jordan measurability of sets is not defined in Mathlib (the existing `Jordan` file concerns the Jordan decomposition of vector measures).

annotations.label: Monotone functions are Riemann integrable

anchor.snippet: If a real-valued function is monotone on the interval

annotations.note: `Monotone.intervalIntegrable` (and `MonotoneOn.intervalIntegrable`) proves integrability of monotone functions on a compact interval, but only for the Bochner/Lebesgue interval integral.

annotations.label: Riemann integrable implies Lebesgue integrable

anchor.snippet: If a real-valued function on

annotations.note: No direct theorem linking `BoxIntegral.Integrable` to `MeasureTheory.IntegrableOn` (Lebesgue) was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Uniform convergence preserves Riemann integrability

anchor.snippet: is a uniformly convergent sequence on

annotations.note: Mathlib proves convergence of `intervalIntegral` under uniform convergence of continuous functions, but not the preservation of Riemann integrability per se.

annotations.label: Riemann integral for vector-valued functions

anchor.snippet: The integral is defined component-wise

annotations.note: `BoxIntegral.HasIntegral` already takes values in an arbitrary normed space `F`, so vector-valued Riemann integration is subsumed directly (not component-wise).

annotations.label: Improper Riemann integral

anchor.snippet: The simplest possible extension is to define such an integral as a limit

annotations.note: Mathlib formalises improper integrals as limits of `intervalIntegral` over expanding intervals (e.g. `intervalIntegral_tendsto_integral_Ioi/Iic`), in the Lebesgue setting.

annotations.label: Sign function improper integral

anchor.snippet: consider the sign function

annotations.note: No example computing/discussing the improper integral of `Real.sign` was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Uniform convergence fails on unbounded intervals

anchor.snippet: take f n ( x ) to be n −1 on

annotations.note: This counterexample about uniform convergence on unbounded intervals is not formalized.

annotations.label: Riemann integrable iff bounded with discontinuity set of measure zero

anchor.snippet: a function f defined on a bounded interval is Riemann-integrable if and only if it is bounded

annotations.note: The Lebesgue criterion (bounded + discontinuities of measure zero) for Riemann integrability is not formalized in Mathlib.
